skill.name: git
skill.description: Guides Git branch checks, conventional commit preparation, pull request readiness, and repository status review.
skill.summary: Manage Git workflow steps with branch, commit, PR, and repository health guidance.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static external command detections are true positives for documented git command examples, but they are expected for a Git workflow skill. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are false positives at the cited lines; no cryptographic API or weak algorithm use is present. No prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: Git Command Examples Can Modify Repository State
low_findings.description: The fenced bash block shows git branch, git status, and git commit examples. These commands are legitimate for this skill, but running the commit example can change the current repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly visible in the cited block. They match the skill purpose and do not show malicious intent, but git commit can create repository changes.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography detections at the cited lines do not show cryptographic functions, hashing APIs, encryption APIs, or weak algorithm names. The lines contain skill description text, a PR workflow filename, branch format guidance, and a commit message placeholder.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were inspected and contain no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection. This strongly supports dismissing the high-severity static detections.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell-form git command examples inside a markdown code block. This is expected for Git workflow assistance, but users should approve commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is present and semantically tied to Git operations. There is no evidence of hidden execution, command obfuscation, or exfiltration.

content.user_title: Manage Git Workflows with Review Checks
content.value_statement: Teams lose time when branches, commits, and pull requests follow inconsistent rules. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Git workflow guidance for cleaner reviews.
actual_capabilities: Guides branch naming checks for common Git workflow prefixes.
Provides repository status and health check steps.
Outlines pull request preparation and validation tasks.
Maps branch prefixes to semantic release impact.
limitations: Does not include executable helper scripts or automation files.
Does not validate remote repository settings or branch protection rules.
Does not replace human review of generated commit or PR text.
Requires user approval before any Git command changes repository state.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Check branch naming, inspect repository status, and align work with team Git conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Commit Messages
use_cases.description: Draft conventional commit messages with type, scope, body, and footer guidance.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Use PR preparation and validation prompts before opening or updating a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Git Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current Git status and explain what needs attention before I commit.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Branch Naming
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my current branch name follows the expected type and descriptive slug format.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Conventional Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a conventional commit message for my staged changes. Include a clear type, scope, summary, body, and footer if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this branch for pull request readiness. Check status, commit quality, release impact, and missing validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my branch before I open a pull request.
output_examples.output: A concise readiness review with branch naming feedback, repository status concerns, commit quality notes, and final PR checklist items.

output_examples.input: Help me write a commit message for these staged changes.
output_examples.output: A conventional commit draft with a type, optional scope, short description, body summary, and any required footer guidance.

output_examples.input: Tell me whether this change affects semantic release.
output_examples.output: A release impact assessment based on branch prefix and commit type, with notes for patch, minor, or no release impact.

best_practices: Review generated Git commands before execution, especially commands that create commits.
Keep branch names short, descriptive, and aligned with the listed prefixes.
Use conventional commit types consistently so release impact is easier to understand.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, tokens, or private credentials into commit or PR prompts.
Do not run generated Git commands without checking the target branch and staged changes.
Do not use this skill as the only approval step for production releases.

faq.question: Does this skill run Git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents workflow steps and command examples. Users should approve any command before execution.

faq.question: Can it create conventional commit messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides structure for type, scope, description, body, and footer fields.

faq.question: Does it support pull request preparation?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers pull request description generation, validation, and checklist review.

faq.question: Can it enforce branch protection rules?
faq.answer: No. It can guide checks, but repository settings must be configured in the Git hosting platform.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It can be used with private repositories, but users should avoid sharing secrets or sensitive business details.

faq.question: Which AI coding tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
